<div style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:12pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">OVERVIEW</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citigroup&#8217;s history dates back to the founding of the City Bank of New York in 1812. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citigroup is a global diversified financial services holding company whose businesses provide consumers, corporations, governments and institutions with a broad, yet focused, range of financial products and services, including consumer banking and credit, corporate and investment banking, securities brokerage, trade and securities services and wealth management. Citi does business in nearly 160 countries and jurisdictions.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citi&#8217;s vision is to be the preeminent banking partner for institutions with cross-border needs, a global leader in wealth management and a valued personal bank in the U.S.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Throughout this report, &#8220;Citigroup,&#8221; &#8220;Citi&#8221; and &#8220;the Company&#8221; refer to Citigroup Inc. and its consolidated subsidiaries. All &#8220;Note&#8221; references correspond to the Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements herein, unless otherwise indicated. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For a list of certain terms and acronyms used in this Annual Report on Form 10-K and other Citigroup presentations, see &#8220;Glossary of Terms and Acronyms&#8221; at the end of this report. </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Additional Information</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Additional information about Citigroup is available on Citi&#8217;s website at www.citigroup.com. Citigroup&#8217;s annual reports on Form 10-K, quarterly reports on Form 10-Q, current reports on Form 8-K and proxy statements, as well as other filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) are available free of charge through Citi&#8217;s website by clicking on &#8220;SEC Filings&#8221; under the &#8220;Investors&#8221; tab. The SEC&#8217;s website also contains these filings and other information regarding Citi at www.sec.gov. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">,</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Wealth</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">,</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New in countries with highly inflationary economies reported within </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Prior periods were conformed to reflect this change in presentation.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div Prior periods were conformed to reflect this change in presentation.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><table style="border-collapse:collapse;display:inline-table;margin-bottom:5pt;vertical-align:text-bottom;width:100.000%"><tr><td style="width:1.0%"/><td style="width:98.900%"/><td style="width:0.1%"/></tr><tr style="height:8pt"><td colspan="3" style="border-bottom:2pt solid #000000;padding:0 1pt"/></tr></table></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Please see &#8220;Risk Factors&#8221; below for a discussion of material risks and uncertainties that could impact Citi&#8217;s businesses, results of operations and financial condition.</span></div><div><table style="border-collapse:collapse;display:inline-table;margin-bottom:5pt;vertical-align:text-bottom;width:100.000%"><tr><td style="width:1.0%"/><td style="width:98.900%"/><td style="width:0.1%"/></tr><tr style="height:5pt"><td colspan="3" style="border-bottom:2pt solid #000000;padding:0 1pt"/></tr></table></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div id="iffa471fccb154c4395823fdabb3bda1a_4203"></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Non-GAAP Financial Measures </span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citi prepares its financial statements in accordance with U.S. generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) and also presents certain non-GAAP financial measures (non-GAAP measures) that exclude certain items or otherwise include components that differ from the most directly comparable measures calculated in accordance with U.S. GAAP. These non-GAAP financial measures are not intended to be a substitute for GAAP financial measures and may not be defined or calculated the same way as non-GAAP measures with similar names used by other companies.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citi&#8217;s non-GAAP financial measures in this Form 10-K include the following:</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Revenues excluding the loss on sale due to the held-for-sale accounting treatment related to Citi&#8217;s plan to sell AO Citibank (which was sold on February 18, 2026) in Russia (the Russia-related notable item)</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Expenses excluding a goodwill impairment related to Citi&#8217;s agreement to sell a 25% equity stake in Grupo Financiero Banamex, S.A. de C.V. (Banamex) (the Banamex-related notable item)</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New items</span></div></div></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span as reported, or GAAP, financial results less this notable item. Citi believes its results excluding the Russia-related notable item are useful to investors, industry analysts and others in evaluating Citi&#8217;s results of operations and comparing its operational performance between periods, by providing a meaningful depiction of the underlying fundamentals of period-to-period operating results, particularly given the outsized impact of the item, as well as additional comparability to peer companies.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Citi&#8217;s expenses excluding the Banamex-related notable item represent as reported, or GAAP, financial results less this notable item, within </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Legacy Franchises. For more information on this notable item, see &#8220;Executive Summary&#8221; and &#8220;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Managed Basis&#8212;Legacy Franchises (Managed Basis)&#8221; below. </span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Citi&#8217;s net income and diluted EPS excluding the Russia-related and Banamex-related notable items represent as reported, or GAAP, financial results adjusted for these two notable items. Citi&#8217;s expenses excluding the Banamex-related notable item represent as reported, or GAAP, financial results less the Banamex-related notable item. Citi believes its results excluding the Russia-related and Banamex-related notable items are useful to investors, industry analysts and others in evaluating Citi&#8217;s results of operations and comparing its operational performance between periods, by providing a meaningful depiction of the underlying fundamentals of period-to-period operating results, particularly given the outsized impact of these items, as well as additional comparability to peer companies.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Citi&#8217;s </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> results excluding divestiture-related impacts represent as reported, or GAAP, financial results less the items incurred and recognized that are wholly and necessarily a consequence of actions taken to sell (including through a public offering), dispose of or wind down business activities associated with Citi&#8217;s previously announced exit markets within </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Legacy Franchises. Additionally, Citi&#8217;s Chief Executive Officer, its chief operating decision maker, regularly reviews financial information for </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">on a managed basis that excludes these divestiture-related impacts. For more information on Citi&#8217;s results excluding divestiture-related impacts, see &#8220;Executive Summary&#8221; and &#8220;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Divestiture-Related Impacts (Reconciling Items)&#8221; below.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">For more information on </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> and Corporate Lending revenues excluding gain (loss) on loan hedges, see &#8220;Executive Summary&#8221; and &#8220;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8221; below. Citi believes that </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> and Corporate Lending revenues excluding gain (loss) on loan hedges are useful to investors, industry analysts and others because the gain (loss) on loan hedges are independent of </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> and Corporate Lending&#8217;s core operations and not indicative of the performance of the business operations.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">For more information on TCE, RoTCE and TBVPS, see &#8220;Capital Resources&#8212;Tangible Common Equity, Book Value Per Share, Tangible Book Value Per Share and Return on Equity&#8221; below. TCE, RoTCE and TBVPS are used by management, as well as investors, industry analysts and others, in assessing Citi&#8217;s use of equity. Citi believes TCE and RoTCE are useful to investors, industry analysts and others by providing alternative measures of capital strength and performance. Citi believes TBVPS provides additional useful information about the level of tangible assets in relation to Citi&#8217;s outstanding shares of common stock.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">For more information on non-</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">net interest income, see &#8220;Market Risk&#8212;Non-</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Net Interest Income&#8221; below.</span><span style="color:#002060;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Management uses non-</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> net interest income to assess the performance of Citi&#8217;s non-</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">lending, investing (including asset-liability management) and deposit-raising activities, apart from any volatility associated with such </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8217; activities. Citi believes the use of this non-GAAP measure provides investors, industry analysts and others with an alternative measure to analyze the net interest income trends of Citi&#8217;s lending, investing and deposit-raising activities, by providing a meaningful depiction of the underlying fundamentals of period-to-period operating results of those activities; improved visibility into management decisions and their impacts on operational performance; and additional comparability to peer companies.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Included in Citi&#8217;s reported revenues was an immaterial increase in divestiture-related revenues of ($176) million in the current year, primarily related to Citi&#8217;s Poland consumer banking business, compared to aggregate divestiture-related revenues of $26 million in the prior year. Accordingly, Citi is not adjusting for these immaterial amounts. </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div></div></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div (Banamex) and its consolidated subsidiaries. clusters within International.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div (Banamex) and its consolidated subsidiaries. For additional information, see &#8220;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Managed Basis&#8212;Legacy Franchises (Managed Basis)&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="padding-left:27pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(4)</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:8.68pt">Primarily represents the remaining two exit countries (Poland and Korea).</span></div><div style="padding-left:27pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(5)</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:8.68pt">Within International, Citi is organized into six clusters: United Kingdom; Japan, Asia North and Australia (JANA); LATAM; Asia South; Europe; and Middle East, Africa and Russia (MEA). clusters within International.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div id="iffa471fccb154c4395823fdabb3bda1a_79"></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:12pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">MANAGEMENT&#8217;S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION </span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:12pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div id="iffa471fccb154c4395823fdabb3bda1a_82"></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div style="-sec-extract:summary"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">EXECUTIVE SUMMARY</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">As described further throughout this Executive Summary, Citi demonstrated improved business performance and made significant progress on its strategic priorities in 2025 and early 2026:</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Citi and its five businesses each achieved positive operating leverage for 2025 for the second consecutive year. Citi&#8217;s positive operating leverage in 2025 was driven by revenue growth of 6% and disciplined expense management, with expenses up 3%.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Citi returned $17.6 billion to common shareholders in the form of share repurchases ($13.3 billion) under its multiyear $20 billion common stock repurchase program and dividends ($4.3 billion). Citi will continue to assess the level of common share repurchases on a quarter-by-quarter basis.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Citi continued to advance its transformation, with over 80% of transformation programs now at or nearly at Citi&#8217;s target state. Additionally, in December 2025, the OCC terminated its July 2024 amendment to Citibank&#8217;s 2020 Consent Order (see &#8220;Citi&#8217;s Multiyear Transformation&#8221; below).</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Citi continued to make progress on its remaining divestitures, including completing the sale of a 25% equity stake in Banamex in 2025 and signing and closing the sale of AO Citibank in Russia to Renaissance Capital (RenCap) on February 18, 2026. For additional information about the sale of AO Citibank and its impacts, see &#8220;Recent Developments&#8221; and &#8220;Managing Global Risk&#8212;Other Risks&#8212;Country Risk&#8212;Russia&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">2025 Results Summary</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Citigroup</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citigroup reported net income of $14.3 billion, or $6.99 per share. This compared to net income of $12.7 billion, or $5.94 per share in the prior year. Results in 2025 included two notable items: </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Russia-related notable item: revenues included a $1.2 billion ($1.1 billion after-tax) loss on sale related to the held-for-sale accounting treatment related to Citi&#8217;s plan to sell AO Citibank in Russia (which was sold on February 18, 2026)</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Banamex-related notable item: expenses included a goodwill impairment of $726 million ($714 million after-tax) related to Citi&#8217;s agreement to sell a 25% equity stake in Grupo Financiero Banamex, S.A. de C.V. (Banamex)</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Excluding the Russia-related notable item and the Banamex-related notable item, net income was $16.1 billion, or $7.97 per share.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Net income increased 13% versus the prior year, driven by higher revenues, partially offset by higher expenses, a higher effective tax rate and higher provisions for credit losses. Citigroup&#8217;s effective tax rate was 27% in 2025 versus 25% in the prior year, driven by the limited tax benefit of the Russia-related and Banamex-related notable items. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citigroup revenues of $85.2 billion in 2025 increased 6% on a reported basis, driven by an increase in net interest income, up 11%, partially offset by lower non-interest revenue, down 4%. The increase in net interest income reflected higher net interest income in</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> and </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Wealth</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, partially offset by lower net interest income in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> (managed basis) and </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">. The decrease in non-interest revenue was driven by declines in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(managed basis), </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> and </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, largely offset by </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Wealth</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> and </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">. Excluding the Russia-related notable item, revenues were $86.4 billion.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citigroup&#8217;s average loans in 2025 were $716 billion, up 5% versus the prior year, largely driven by loan growth in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> and </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">. For additional information about Citi&#8217;s average loans by business, including drivers and loan trends, see each business&#8217;s results of operations and &#8220;Loans Outstanding&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citigroup&#8217;s average deposits in 2025 were approximately $1.4 trillion, up 4% versus the prior year, primarily driven by an increase in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">. For additional information about Citi&#8217;s average deposits by business, including drivers and deposit trends, see each respective business&#8217;s results of operations and &#8220;Liquidity Risk&#8212;Deposits&#8221; below.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Expenses</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citigroup&#8217;s operating expenses of $55.1 billion increased 3% from the prior year, driven by higher compensation and benefits,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">the Banamex-related notable item, higher technology and communications and higher transactional and product servicing expenses, partially offset by lower deposit insurance expenses</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and restructuring charges. The increase in compensation and benefits was driven by</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">performance-related compensation</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and higher severance. The increase in technology and communication was driven by continued investments in transformation and businesses. The increase in transactional and product servicing</span><span style="color:#ee2724;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">was driven by higher volumes in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> and </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Wealth</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, partially offset by </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">. Excluding the Banamex-related notable item, expenses were $54.4 billion. For additional information on Citi&#8217;s transformation investments, see &#8220;Citi&#8217;s Multiyear Transformation&#8221; below.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div></div></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="-sec-extract:summary;text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">8  </span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Provisions</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citi&#8217;s total provisions for credit losses and for benefits and claims were $10.3 billion, reflecting net credit losses of $9.1 billion and a net allowance for credit losses (ACL) build of $1.2 billion. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Net credit losses were up 1%</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">from the prior year, driven by increases in Legacy Franchises in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, largely offset by decreases in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">.</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The net ACL build was driven by changes in the macroeconomic outlook</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and transfer risk. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citi&#8217;s total provisions for credit losses and for benefits and claims in the prior year were $10.1 billion, reflecting net credit losses of $9.0 billion and a net ACL build of $1.1 billion, driven by changes in credit quality, higher net lending activity</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and transfer risk, partially offset by changes in the macroeconomic outlook. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on Citi&#8217;s ACL, see &#8220;Significant Accounting Policies and Significant Estimates&#8212;Citi&#8217;s Allowance for Credit Losses (ACL)&#8221; below. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on Citi&#8217;s consumer and corporate provisions, see each respective segment&#8217;s and </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8217;s results of operations and &#8220;Credit Risk&#8221; below.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Capital</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citigroup&#8217;s Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) Capital ratio was 13.2% as of December 31, 2025, compared to 13.6% as of December 31, 2024, based on the Basel III Standardized Approach for determining risk weighted assets (RWA). The decrease was primarily driven by common share repurchases, an increase in RWA and the payment of common and preferred dividends, partially offset by net income and net beneficial movements in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Accumulated other comprehensive income (AOCI)</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In 2025, Citi repurchased $13.3 billion of common shares and paid $4.3 billion of common dividends (see &#8220;Unregistered Sales of Equity Securities, Repurchases of Equity Securities and Dividends&#8221; below).</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citigroup&#8217;s Supplementary Leverage ratio as of December 31, 2025 was 5.5%, compared to 5.8% as of December 31, 2024. The decrease was driven by an increase in Total Leverage Exposure, partially offset by an increase in Tier 1 Capital. For additional information on Citi&#8217;s capital ratios and related components, see &#8220;Capital Resources&#8221; below. For additional information on capital-related risks, trends and uncertainties, see &#8220;Capital Resources&#8212;Regulatory Capital Standards and Developments&#8221; and &#8220;Risk Factors&#8212;Strategic Risks,&#8221; &#8220;&#8212;Operational Risks&#8221; and &#8220;&#8212;Compliance Risks&#8221; below. </span></div><div style="text-indent:-0.75pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For information on the results of operations for 2025 for each of Citi&#8217;s segments and </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, see &#8220;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">,&#8221; &#8220;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">,&#8221; &#8220;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">,&#8221; &#8220;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Wealth</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">,&#8221; &#8220;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8221; and &#8220;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Managed Basis&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="text-indent:-0.75pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-indent:-0.75pt"><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="text-indent:-0.75pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Macroeconomic and Other Risks and Uncertainties</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Various macroeconomic, geopolitical and regulatory factors have contributed to economic uncertainties in the U.S. and globally, including, but not limited to, those related to various geopolitical challenges, tensions and conflicts; changes in U.S. laws or policies, including those related to trade and tariffs; and lower interest rates. These factors could adversely affect economic growth, unemployment and inflation in the U.S. and other countries and result in volatility and disruptions in financial markets. Such risks and uncertainties could adversely impact Citi&#8217;s clients, customers, businesses, funding costs, provisions and overall results of operations and financial condition during 2026.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For a further discussion of trends, uncertainties and risks that will or could impact Citi&#8217;s segments and </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, results of operations, capital and other financial condition during 2026, see each respective segment&#8217;s and </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8217;s results of operations, &#8220;Risk Factors&#8221; and &#8220;Managing Global Risk,&#8221; including &#8220;Managing Global Risk&#8212;Other Risks&#8212;Country Risk&#8212;Argentina&#8221; below.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div></div></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="-sec-extract:summary;text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">9  </span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:2pt;font-weight:400;line-height:115%">  </span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:2pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">  </span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:2pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">  </span></div><div id="iffa471fccb154c4395823fdabb3bda1a_85"></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">CITI&#8217;S MULTIYEAR TRANSFORMATION</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">As previously disclosed, Citi&#8217;s transformation, including the remediation of its consent orders with the FRB and OCC, is a multiyear endeavor that is not linear. Citi is modernizing and simplifying the Company in order to lead in a dynamic, competitive and digital world. Citi&#8217;s transformation goes beyond remedying regulatory concerns to intentionally transform how the organization operates, and makes investments that not only support current needs, but also benefit the Company over the long term.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citi&#8217;s transformation target outcomes remain focused on changing its business and operating models such that they simultaneously: </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">further strengthen controls, enhance data quality and governance, reduce risk and continue to improve Citi&#8217;s regulatory compliance and its culture</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">enhance Citi&#8217;s value to customers, clients and shareholders</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Transformation efforts of this scale involve significant complexities and uncertainties, including ongoing regulatory challenges and risks. Citi may continue to experience significant challenges in progressing the transformation and satisfying the regulators&#8217; expectations in both sufficiency and timing, particularly with regard to data quality management related to governance and regulatory reporting. The regulators may also identify additional risk and control issues that could result in further regulatory actions. For additional information about these regulatory risks, see &#8220;Risk Factors&#8212;Compliance Risks&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on Citi&#8217;s transformation progress, investments and governance over the last several years, see &#8220;Citi&#8217;s Multiyear Transformation&#8221; in Citi&#8217;s 2024 Annual Report on Form 10-K.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">2025 Transformation Progress</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citi continued to make progress on its transformation in 2025. As of December 31, 2025, over 80% of Citi&#8217;s transformation programs were at or nearly at the Company&#8217;s target state. Progress through 2025 included the following:</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">enhanced and implemented automated controls to further mitigate risk of large erroneous payments in over 90 countries</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">completed migration of committed corporate loans to Citi&#8217;s strategic loans processing platform for North America</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">applied technology solutions leveraging AI to support governance of data reported in key regulatory reports </span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">completed onboarding of wholesale and retail contractual data to two strategic data platforms with built-in controls for accuracy, completeness and timeliness </span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">continued to optimize, modernize and simplify Citi by retiring or replacing 548 applications during 2025 (representing 9% of all applications)</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In 2025, Citi&#8217;s transformation-related expenses increased 14% from the prior year to approximately $3.3 billion, largely driven by increased spending on data, as well as on controls. While Citi&#8217;s transformation investments will remain significant in 2026 and beyond, Citi expects them to decline over time. </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">FRB and OCC Consent Orders Compliance</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citi&#8217;s transformation efforts include implementation of the October 7, 2020 FRB and OCC Consent Orders issued to Citigroup and Citibank, respectively. The 2020 Consent Orders require Citigroup and Citibank to implement extensive targeted action plans and submit quarterly progress reports on a timely and sufficient basis, detailing the results and status of improvements relating principally to various aspects of:</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">enterprise-wide risk management;</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">compliance risk management;</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">data quality management related to governance; and </span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">internal controls. </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Although there are no restrictions on Citi&#8217;s ability to serve its clients, the 2020 OCC Consent Order requires Citibank to obtain prior approval of any significant new acquisition, including any portfolio or business acquisition, excluding ordinary course transactions. For additional information about the requirements under the 2020 Consent Orders, see Citi&#8217;s October 9, 2020 Form 8-K.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In 2024 the FRB entered into a Civil Money Penalty Consent Order with Citigroup in the amount of approximately $61 million, having found that Citigroup had ongoing deficiencies related to its data quality management program and inadequate measures for managing and controlling its data quality risks; and the OCC entered into a Civil Money Penalty Consent Order with Citibank, a wholly owned subsidiary of Citigroup, in the amount of $75 million, having found that Citibank had failed to make sufficient and sustainable progress toward achieving compliance with the OCC&#8217;s 2020 Consent Order. The OCC and Citibank also entered into an amendment to the October 7, 2020 OCC Consent Order (the Amendment), which the OCC terminated on December 18, 2025. For additional information on the 2024 Consent Orders and the Amendment, see Citi&#8217;s July 10, 2024 Form 8-K.</span></div><div style="text-indent:21.6pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citi continues to work constructively with the FRB and OCC and provide additional information regarding its plans and progress to both regulators on an ongoing basis.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div></div></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">10  </span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Transformation Governance</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citi has built an organization and infrastructure to manage, guide and support its transformation, which spans all businesses and functions to ensure consistency. Additionally, the Citigroup and Citibank Boards of Directors each formed a Transformation Oversight Committee, an ad hoc committee of each Board, to provide oversight of Citi&#8217;s efforts to improve its risk and control environment and management&#8217;s remediation efforts under the consent orders.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">While every member of Citi&#8217;s executive management team, or EMT, is involved in the transformation and plays a key, direct role in its implementation, Citi&#8217;s CEO has taken a leading role in managing the effort. As part of this effort, Citi&#8217;s CEO has assembled a team consisting of long-tenured employees and new hires from across various disciplines and areas of expertise and experience, along with representatives from each of Citi&#8217;s businesses and functions, to lead the various transformation programs. Citi is focusing its most senior talent on this effort and has a detailed, integrated approach to execute on the transformation. Citi&#8217;s Transformation Steering Committee, chaired by Citi&#8217;s CEO, sets the overall direction for the transformation and communicates progress to the Citigroup Board of Directors, as well as seeks input and feedback from the Board.</span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:2pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">   </span></div><div id="iffa471fccb154c4395823fdabb3bda1a_3910"></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:12pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">RECENT DEVELOPMENTS</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On February 18, 2026, Citi signed and closed the sale of AO Citibank, Citi&#8217;s former Russian subsidiary, to RenCap. The transaction resulted in Citi&#8217;s full exit from its operations in Russia and included all remaining businesses, as well as approximately 800 employees. The divestiture of the remaining business operations is expected to provide an estimated benefit to Citi&#8217;s Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital in the first quarter of 2026 of approximately $4 billion, primarily driven by the deconsolidation of associated risk-weighted assets, a reduction in disallowed deferred tax assets (DTA) and the release of associated currency translation adjustment (CTA) loss. While a benefit in the first quarter of 2026, the cumulative impact of the $1.6 billion CTA loss is regulatory capital neutral to Citi. For additional information, see &#8220;Managing Global Risk&#8212;Other Risks&#8212;Country Risk&#8212;Russia&#8221; below and Note 2.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div></div></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">11  </span></div></div></div><div id="iffa471fccb154c4395823fdabb3bda1a_88"></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="margin-bottom:10pt;margin-top:8pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:12pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">RESULTS OF OPERATIONS</span></div><div><span style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(1)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;Effective January 1, 2025, certain transaction processing fees paid by Citi, primarily to credit card networks, reported within </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Wealth </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">All Other</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Legacy Franchises (Mexico Consumer/SBMM and Asia Consumer), which were previously presented within </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Other operating</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> expenses, are presented as contra-revenue within </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Commissions and fees</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> reported in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Non-interest revenue</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">. Prior periods were conformed to reflect this change in presentation.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(2)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;The return on average common stockholders&#8217; equity is calculated using net income less preferred stock dividends divided by average common stockholders&#8217; equity. The return on average total Citigroup stockholders&#8217; equity is calculated using net income divided by average Citigroup stockholders&#8217; equity.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(3)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;RoTCE and TBVPS are non-GAAP financial measures. For information on RoTCE and TBVPS, see &#8220;Capital Resources&#8212;Tangible Common Equity, Book Value Per Share, Tangible Book Value Per Share and Return on Equity&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(4)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;The operating leverage ratio represents the year-over-year growth rate in basis points (bps) of </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Total revenues, net of interest expense</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> less the year-over-year growth rate of </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Total operating expenses</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">. Positive operating leverage indicates that the revenue growth rate was greater than the expense growth rate.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:112%">(5)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;Citi&#8217;s binding CET1 Capital ratios were derived under the Basel III Standardized Approach and Total Capital ratios were derived under the Advanced Approaches framework for all periods presented. Citi&#8217;s binding Tier 1 Capital ratios were derived under the Basel III Advanced Approaches framework as of December 31, 2025 and were derived under the Standardized Approach as of December 31, 2024, 2023, 2022 and 2021.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:115%">(6)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;The dividend payout ratio represents dividends declared per common share as a percentage of net income per diluted share.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(7)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;The total payout ratio represents the total of common dividends declared plus common share repurchases as a percentage of net income available to common shareholders (</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Net income</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> less preferred dividends). See &#8220;Consolidated Statement of Changes in Stockholders&#8217; Equity,&#8221; Note 11 and &#8220;Equity Security Repurchases&#8221; below for the component details. </span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">13  </span></div></div></div><div id="iffa471fccb154c4395823fdabb3bda1a_3938"></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">TTS provides an integrated suite of tailored cash management, payments and trade and working capital solutions to multinational corporations, financial institutions and public sector organizations. </span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Securities Services connects investors and issuers across global markets, providing a comprehensive product offering, including on-the-ground local market expertise, post-trade technologies, customized data solutions and a wide range of securities services solutions that can be tailored to meet clients&#8217; needs.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> revenue is generated primarily from spreads and fees associated with these activities. </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> earns spread revenue on deposits, as well as interest on loans. Revenue generated from these activities is primarily recorded in Net interest income in the table below. </span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Fee income is earned for assisting clients with transactional services and clearing. Revenue generated from these activities is recorded in Commissions and fees. Revenue is also generated from assets under custody and administration (AUC/AUA) and is primarily recorded in Administration and other fiduciary fees. For additional information on these types of revenues, see Note 5. </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> revenues reflect the impact of a revenue sharing arrangement with </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Corporate Lending, for </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> products sold to Corporate Lending clients. This generally results in a reduction in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> reported revenue recorded in All other as part of Non-interest revenue in the table below.
