style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">18  </span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">2025 vs. 2024</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Net income</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> of $7.1 billion increased 9%.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Revenues </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">increased 8%, driven by growth in TTS and Securities Services, which included the positive impact of the Russia-related notable item of $356 million. </span></div><div style="text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Net interest income increased 12%, driven by an increase in average deposit balances and spreads. Average deposits increased 7%, driven by growth in TTS and Securities Services, with growth across both International and North America, largely driven by an increase in operating deposits. Non-interest revenue increased 1%, driven by fee growth of 6% and the positive impact of the Russia-related notable item, offset by higher lending revenue share with </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Corporate Lending. </span></div><div style="text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">TTS revenues increased 6%, driven by a 12% increase in net interest income, partially offset by a 12% decrease in non-interest revenue. The increase in net interest income was driven by higher deposit spreads and average deposit balances, up 6%. The decrease in non-interest revenue</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">was driven by higher lending revenue share, partially offset by higher fees.</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Fee revenue increased 5%, reflecting continued growth in underlying fee drivers, including an increase in cross-border transaction value of 10%, an increase in U.S. dollar clearing volume of 5% and an increase in commercial card spend volume of 1%.</span></div><div style="text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Securities Services revenues increased 15%, driven by a 19% increase in non-interest revenue and 11% increase in net interest income. The increase in non-interest revenue was driven by the positive impact of the Russia-related notable item and fee growth of 7%, which benefited from higher assets under custody and administration</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and client activity, as well as a mark-to-market gain</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">partially offset by higher lending revenue share. Assets under custody and administration increased 24%, which included the benefit of higher market valuations, deepening share of existing client wallet and the onboarding of new clients and assets. The increase in net interest income was driven by higher average deposits, which increased 12%, partially offset by lower deposit spreads. </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="text-indent:13.5pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Expenses </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">increased 2%, primarily driven by higher compensation and benefits expense,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">including performance-related compensation. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Provisions </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">were $454 million, reflecting a net ACL build of $398 million, and net credit losses of $56 million. The net ACL build was primarily driven by transfer risk. Provisions were $276 million in the prior year, reflecting a net ACL build of $228 million, driven by transfer risk,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and net credit losses of $48 million.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#0d0d0d;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on Citi&#8217;s ACL, see &#8220;Significant Accounting Policies and Significant Estimates&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8217; corporate credit portfolio, see &#8220;Managing Global Risk&#8212;Credit Risk&#8212;Corporate Credit&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on trends in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8217; deposits and loans, see &#8220;Managing Global Risk&#8212;Credit Risk&#8212;Loans&#8221; and &#8220;Managing Global Risk&#8212;Liquidity Risk&#8212;Deposits&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information about trends, uncertainties and risks related to future results of the businesses, see &#8220;Executive Summary&#8221; above and &#8220;Managing Global Risk&#8212;Other Risks&#8212;Country Risk&#8212;Argentina&#8221; below.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div></div></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">19  </span></div></div></div><div id="iffa471fccb154c4395823fdabb3bda1a_106"></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:12pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">MARKETS</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> includes Fixed Income Markets and Equity Markets and provides corporate, institutional and public sector clients around the world with a full range of sales and trading services across equities, foreign exchange, rates, spread products and commodities. The range of services includes market-making across asset classes, risk management solutions, financing and prime brokerage.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citi assesses its </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> business performance on a total revenues basis, as security inventory is often hedged by derivative instruments, creating offsetting gains and losses across revenue lines. As an example, securities that generate Net interest income may be hedged by derivative instruments, which are reported under Principal transactions within Non-interest revenue.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">As a market maker, </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> facilitates transactions by holding inventory to meet client demand, with resulting gains or losses largely recorded as </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Principal transactions</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">. Fee revenue is generated from services such as trading, financing, </span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">brokerage, securitization and underwriting. &#8220;Other&#8221; revenue includes gains (losses) on AFS debt and equity securities (non-trading), and other non-recurring items. Revenue generated from all of these activities is primarily recorded in Non-interest revenue in the table below. </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> revenues also reflect the impact of a revenue sharing arrangement with </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Corporate Lending, for </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> products sold to Corporate Lending clients. This generally results in a reduction in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> reported revenue recorded in All other.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Net interest income includes interest and dividends on securities held and interest on long- and short-term debt, secured funding transactions, deposits, loans and funding costs.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">maintains an international presence supported by trading floors in nearly 80 countries and Citi&#8217;s proprietary network in over 90 countries and jurisdictions.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span><br/></span></div></div></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><table style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(1)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;Investment banking fees are primarily composed of underwriting, Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(2)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> revenues reflect the impact of a revenue sharing arrangement with </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Corporate Lending, for </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> products sold to Corporate Lending clients. This generally results in a reduction in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> reported revenue.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(3)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;Citi assesses its </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> business performance on a total revenue basis, as offsets may occur across revenue line items. For example, securities that generate </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Net interest income</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> may be risk managed by derivatives that are recorded in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Principal transactions</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> revenue within </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Non-interest revenue</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">. For a description of the composition of these revenue line items, see Notes 4, 5 and 6.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(4)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;Management uses this information in reviewing the segment&#8217;s results and believes it is useful to investors concerning underlying segment performance and trends.</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(5)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;Excludes loans that are carried at fair value for all periods. </span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">NM  Not meaningful</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"></div></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">21  </span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">2025 vs. 2024</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Net income</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> of $5.9 billion increased 19%.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Revenues</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> increased 11%, driven by higher revenues in both Fixed Income Markets and Equity Markets.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Fixed Income Markets revenue of $16.2 billion increased 10%, reflecting higher revenues in Rates and Currencies and Spread Products and Other Fixed Income. Rates and Currencies revenues increased 12%, driven by higher Rates and foreign exchange revenues on increased client activity, partially offset by higher lending revenue share</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">with </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Corporate Lending.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Spread Products and Other Fixed Income revenues increased 5%, driven by higher financing activity, with an 18% increase in average loans in Spread Products, and continued optimization of the balance sheet, largely offset by lower commodities revenues.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Equity Markets revenue was $5.7 billion, up 13%, due to increased market volatility and client activity, with prime balances up over 50%. The increase in Equity Markets revenue was driven by higher Equity Derivatives and Prime Services revenues, partially offset by non-recurrence of prior-year gains related to the Visa B share exchange in Equity Derivatives. Equity Cash revenues were marginally lower following a very strong performance in 2024.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Expenses</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> of $14.1 billion increased 7%, primarily driven by higher compensation and benefits, including performance-related compensation and higher technology, transactional and legal expenses.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Provisions </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">were $237 million, reflecting net credit losses of $206 million, and a net ACL build of $31 million. Net credit losses were driven by charge-offs in Spread Products. Provisions were $463 million in the prior year, reflecting a net ACL build of $295 million, primarily driven by changes in credit quality and in the macroeconomic outlook, and net credit losses of $168 million.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#0d0d0d;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on Citi&#8217;s ACL, see &#8220;Significant Accounting Policies and Significant Estimates&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8217;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">corporate credit portfolio, see &#8220;Managing Global Risk&#8212;Credit Risk&#8212;Corporate Credit&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on trends in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8217; deposits and loans, see &#8220;Managing Global Risk&#8212;Credit Risk&#8212;Loans&#8221; and &#8220;Managing Global Risk&#8212;Liquidity Risk&#8212;Deposits&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information about trends, uncertainties and risks related to future results of the businesses, see &#8220;Executive Summary&#8221; above and &#8220;Managing Global Risk&#8212;Other Risks&#8212;Country Risk&#8212;Argentina&#8221; below.</span></div></div></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">22  </span></div></div></div><div id="iffa471fccb154c4395823fdabb3bda1a_109"></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:12pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">BANKING</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> includes Investment Banking (</span><span style="color:#111111;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Debt Capital Markets (DCM), Equity Capital Markets (ECM) and Advisory sub-businesses) </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and Corporate Lending:</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Investment Banking supports clients&#8217; capital-raising needs to help strengthen and grow their businesses, including equity and debt capital markets strategic financing solutions and loan syndication structuring, as well as advisory services related to mergers and acquisitions, divestitures, restructurings and corporate defense activities. </span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Corporate Lending consists of corporate and commercial banking, serving as the conduit for Citi&#8217;s product suite to clients.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In addition to earning net interest spread revenue on its Corporate Lending activities, </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> primarily generates investment banking fees, composed of underwriting, advisory, loan syndication structuring and other related financing activity. For additional information on these types of revenues, see Note 5.</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> Banking </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">revenues also reflect the impact of a revenue sharing arrangement with </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Corporate Lending for Investment Banking, </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> and </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> products sold to Corporate Lending clients. This generally results in an increase in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> reported revenue. The revenue share to </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Corporate Lending is</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">recorded in All other as part of Non-interest revenue in the table below.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">maintains an international presence leveraging a global network of bankers supporting over 90 countries.</span></div></div></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><table style="border-collapse:collapse;display:inline-table;margin-bottom:5pt;vertical-align:text-bottom;width:100.000%"><tr><td 0;text-align:right;vertical-align:bottom"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:9pt;font-weight:700;line-height:100%">%</span></td><td colspan="2" style="background-color:#cceeff;border-bottom:2pt solid #000;padding:2px 0 2px 1pt;text-align:right;vertical-align:bottom"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:9pt;font-weight:400;line-height:100%">1.42&#160;</span></td><td style="background-color:#cceeff;border-bottom:2pt solid #000;padding:2px 1pt 2px 0;text-align:right;vertical-align:bottom"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:9pt;font-weight:400;line-height:100%">%</span></td><td colspan="2" style="background-color:#cceeff;border-bottom:2pt solid #000;padding:2px 0 2px 1pt;text-align:right;vertical-align:bottom"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:9pt;font-weight:400;line-height:100%">1.59&#160;</span></td><td style="background-color:#cceeff;border-bottom:2pt solid #000;padding:2px 1pt 2px 0;text-align:right;vertical-align:bottom"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:9pt;font-weight:400;line-height:100%">%</span></td><td colspan="3" 1pt"/></tr></table></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(1)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> revenues reflect the impact of a revenue sharing arrangement with </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8212;Corporate Lending for Investment Banking, </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Markets</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> and </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> products sold to Corporate Lending clients. This generally results in an increase in </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> reported revenue.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(2)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;Credit derivatives are used to economically hedge a portion of the corporate loan portfolio that includes both accrual loans and loans at fair value. Gain (loss) on loan hedges includes the mark-to-market on the credit derivatives, partially offset by the mark-to-market on the loans in the portfolio that are at fair value. Hedges on accrual loans reflect the mark-to-market on credit derivatives used to economically hedge the corporate loan accrual portfolio. The fixed premium costs of these hedges are netted against the corporate lending revenues to reflect the cost of credit protection. Citigroup&#8217;s results of operations excluding the impact of gain (loss) on loan hedges are non-GAAP financial measures. </span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(3)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;Management uses this information in reviewing the segment&#8217;s results and believes it is useful to investors concerning underlying segment performance and trends.</span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(4)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;Excludes loans that are carried at fair value for all periods. </span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">NM  Not meaningful</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">24  </span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The discussion of the results of operations for </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> below excludes (where noted) the impact of any gain (loss) on hedges of accrual loans, which are non-GAAP financial measures. For a reconciliation of these metrics to the reported results, see the table above.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">2025 vs. 2024</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Net income</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> of $2.3 billion increased 53%. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Revenues</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">increased 32%, driven by growth in Investment Banking</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and Corporate Lending (excluding the impact of losses on loan hedges) and a lower mark-to-market loss on loan hedges (a $118 million loss versus a $180 million loss in the prior year). Excluding the impact of gain (loss) on loan hedges, </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> revenues increased 31%. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Investment Banking revenues increased 22%,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">driven by a 20% increase</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">in investment banking fees across Advisory,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">DCM and ECM with a larger overall market wallet and overall wallet share gains. Advisory fees increased 53%, with growth across several sectors and higher sponsor-led activity. DCM fees were up 5%,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">driven by growth in leveraged finance</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and investment-grade debt,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">partially offset by a decline in investment-grade loan activity.</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">ECM fees</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">were up 2%, driven by strength in IPOs</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and convertibles,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">amid favorable equity market conditions, offset by lower follow-on activity.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Corporate Lending revenues increased 47%, including the impact of gain (loss) on loan hedges. Excluding the impact of gain (loss) on loan hedges, Corporate Lending revenues increased 42%, driven by the impact of higher lending revenue share from </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Services</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span investments made in the business, was offset by a reduction in other operating expenses.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New The net ACL build was driven by changes in credit quality, changes in the macroeconomic outlook and higher net lending activity.</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Provisions were a benefit of $224 million in the prior year, reflecting a net ACL release of $373 million, driven by improved macroeconomic conditions</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and transfer risk, and net credit losses of $149 million.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on Citi&#8217;s ACL, see &#8220;Significant Accounting Policies and Significant Estimates&#8221; below. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Banking</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8217;s</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">corporate credit portfolio, see &#8220;Managing Global Risk&#8212;Credit Risk&#8212;Corporate Credit&#8221; above and &#8220;Managing Global Risk&#8212;Other Risks&#8212;Country Risk&#8212;Argentina&#8221; below.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div></div></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">25  </span></div></div></div><div id="iffa471fccb154c4395823fdabb3bda1a_112"></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="-sec-extract:summary"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:12pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">WEALTH</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">As of December 31, 2025, </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Wealth</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> includes the Private Bank, Citigold and Wealth at Work and provides financial and advisory services to a range of client segments consisting of ultra-high net worth, high net worth and affluent clients. These services include banking, investment, lending and custody product offerings in approximately 20 countries, including the U.S. and four wealth management centers: Singapore, Hong Kong, the UAE and London:</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New </span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</span><span clients through elevated product offerings and financial relationships. </span></div><div style="padding-left:18pt;text-indent:-18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New accounting and asset management firms) through tailored solutions.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span Net interest income is mainly driven by interest earned on client deposits and tailored lending solutions, including mortgages, margin lending, personal, small business and other loans, and international credit cards. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Fee income is primarily generated from asset-based advisory and management fees, as well as transaction-related fees from client investment activity across brokerage, structured products, foreign exchange and banking services. For additional information on these types of revenues, see Note 5.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Effective as of the first quarter of 2026, Citi transferred its Retail Banking business from </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">to </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Wealth</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, and the remaining </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> businesses, including Branded Cards and Retail Services, were integrated into a new </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">U.S. Consumer Cards (USCC)</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> segment. style="min-height:36pt;width:100%"><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">2025 vs. 2024 </span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Net income</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> of $1.5 billion increased 49%.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Revenues</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> increased 14%, driven by growth across Citigold, the Private Bank</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and Wealth at Work. Net interest income increased 17%,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">driven by higher deposit spreads, partially offset by lower mortgage spreads</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and lower average deposit balances. Non-interest revenue increased 10%, driven by higher investment fee revenues</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and a gain on sale of an alternative investment fund platform, partially offset by the loss of fee revenue from a sale of a trust business in the third quarter of 2025. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Client balances increased 9%, primarily driven by higher client investment assets, up 14%. The increase in client investment assets was driven by higher market valuations</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and NNIA generation ($44 billion for the last 12 months), which represented 8% organic growth, partially offset by the sale of a trust business in the third quarter of 2025.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Average deposits decreased 1%, driven by lower deposits in Citigold in North America, as client transfers from </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> (including $15 billion of net transfers during the last 12 months) and net new deposits were more than offset by outflows, including a shift from deposits to higher-yielding investments. Average loans were unchanged, with growth in margin lending offset by transfers of certain relationships and associated mortgage loans to </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> from </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Wealth</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Private Bank revenues increased 12%, driven by higher deposit spreads, the gain on sale of an alternative investments fund platform</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and higher investment fee revenues, partially offset by lower mortgage spreads. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Citigold revenues increased 17%, driven by higher deposit spreads, higher investment fee revenues</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and higher lending spreads, partially offset by lower average loan balances</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and lower average deposit balances. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Wealth at Work revenues increased 6%, driven by higher deposit spreads</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and higher investment fee revenues, primarily offset by lower mortgage spreads. </span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Expenses</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> increased 3%, largely driven by</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">higher technology expenses, higher transactional and product servicing expenses</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and higher compensation and benefit expenses, as higher performance-related compensation and higher severance were partially offset by lower salaries due to lower headcount.</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Provisions </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">were $140 million, reflecting net credit losses of $170 million, and a net ACL release of $30 million. Net credit losses were primarily driven by international credit cards and write-downs of certain mortgage loans to their collateral value due to the impact of the California wildfires. Provisions were a benefit of $126 million in the prior year, reflecting a net ACL release of $247 million, driven by a refinement of loss assumptions in the margin lending portfolio and changes in the macroeconomic outlook, and net credit losses of $121 million.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on Citi&#8217;s ACL, see &#8220;Significant Accounting Policies and Significant Estimates&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on BANKING</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="width:100.000%"><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.666%"><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:14.5pt">Retail Banking includes traditional banking services, including deposits, mortgages and other lending products, for retail and small business customers. Retail Banking branches are concentrated in six key metropolitan areas: New York, Los Angeles, San Francisco, Chicago, Miami and Washington, D.C.</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> revenue is primarily generated from net interest income, driven by secured and unsecured consumer loans, including credit card and mortgage lending, as well as spread income on deposits. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Fee revenue is primarily generated through credit card activities, including interchange revenue and other card-related fees. Fee revenue reflects the impact of customer reward programs, partner payments within the credit card businesses and retail banking-related fees. For additional information on these types of revenues, see Note 5.</span></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Effective as of the first quarter of 2026, Citi transferred its Retail Banking business from </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">to </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Wealth</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">, and the remaining </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">USPB</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> businesses, including Branded Cards and Retail Services, were integrated into a new </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">U.S. Consumer Cards (USCC)</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> segment. For additional information, see &#8220;Overview&#8221; above.</span></div></div></div><div><span><br/></span></div><div><table style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">2025 vs. 2024</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> of $3.1 billion increased 124%. </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Revenues</span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> increased 5%,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">driven by growth in Branded Cards and Retail Banking,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">partially offset by a decline in Retail Services. Net interest income increased 6%,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">driven by higher loan spreads</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and interest-earning balances</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">in Branded Cards, as well as higher deposit spreads in Retail Banking,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">partially offset by lower interest-earning loan balances</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and spreads in Retail Services.</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Non-interest revenue decreased 43%,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">driven by higher rewards costs in Branded Cards</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and higher partner payment accruals in Retail Services,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">partially offset by higher gross interchange fees in Branded Cards.</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Branded Cards revenues increased by 8%, driven by higher loan spreads and interest-earning balances, up 6%, as well as higher gross interchange fees,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">partially offset by higher rewards costs. Branded Cards average loans increased 5%.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Retail Services revenues decreased 6%, driven by higher partner payment accruals, as well as lower net interest income</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">due to lower interest-earning loan balances</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and spreads.</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Retail Services average loans decreased 2%.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Retail Banking revenues increased 21%,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">primarily driven by the impact of higher deposit spreads. Average deposits decreased 2%, as net new deposits were more than offset by client transfers to </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Wealth </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(including $15 billion of net transfers during the last 12 months). </span></div><div><span><br/></span></div></div><div style="display:inline-block;max-width:6.666%;min-width:5.666%;vertical-align:top"></div><div style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:top;width:46.668%"><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Expenses </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">increased 1%, driven by higher transactional and marketing expenses</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">to support customer acquisition and engagement, largely offset by lower compensation and benefits due to lower headcount.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Provisions </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">were $7.2 billion, reflecting net credit losses of $7.4 billion, and a net ACL release of $220 million. Net credit losses were down 2%,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">driven by improved credit performance in Retail Services,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">partially offset by an increase for loan growth in Branded Cards.</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The net ACL release was driven by changes in credit quality,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">largely offset by changes in the macroeconomic outlook.</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Provisions were $8.6 billion in the prior year, reflecting net credit losses of $7.6 billion, driven by Branded Cards and Retail Services,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and a net ACL build of $1.0 billion, driven by changes in credit quality</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and higher volume,</span><span style="color:#ff0000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">partially offset by changes in the macroeconomic outlook.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For additional information on Citi&#8217;s ACL, see &#8220;Significant Accounting Policies and Significant Estimates&#8221; below.</span></div><div style="text-indent:18pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For continue to be guided by several</span></div><div><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">factors, including, among other
